sanskrit:
बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्या आत्मानं नियम्य च।
शब्दादीन् विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च।।५१।।
transliteration:
buddhyā viśuddhayā yukto dhṛtyātmānaṃ niyamya ca |
śabdādīn viṣayāṃs tyaktvā rāgadveṣau vyudasya ca ||51||
literal_meaning: With the intelligence completely purified, endowed with firm resolve, having restrained the self, having abandoned the sense-objects such as sound, having cast off attachment and aversion…
contextual_meaning: Krishna outlines the cognitive-behavioral protocol for self-realization: cleanse perception, strengthen executive control, and systematically extinguish conditioned likes/dislikes that keep the mind oscillating between craving and hatred.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative detachment—intellect is sharpened to discriminate between stimulus and emotional reaction, preparing for deeper meditative absorption.
trigger_scenario: A professional who has just deleted social-media apps, feels the itch to check the phone, notices the urge without acting on it, and realizes the urge is not the self.
anchor_text: Purify your mind, master your senses, and drop attachment and aversion to find inner stillness.
